[IC] (0.69) INT-09581: **IR** — three meanings simultaneously: (1) Intermediate Representation, the PHASE-IR graph format. (2) Intention/Result — a unit of accountability, a promise. (3) "Ir" (Spanish) — to go. Action. Movement. A dropped IR pair is a broken promise AND a stopped action.  <- browser/src/services/terminal/prompts/patois.md

[CON] (0.75) INT-09645: When `requires_rationale: true`, agents must include a text explanation for **every request**. The gate enforcer checks that a rationale is present. If missing, the disposition is `HOLD` until the agent provides one.  <- docs/global-commons/ethics.md
[CON] (0.75) INT-09646: A company enables `requires_rationale: true` for their production environment. An agent requests to delete 1,000 old log files. The gate enforcer requires a rationale. The agent provides: "Freeing 50GB disk space. Logs older than 90 days per retention policy." The human reviews the rationale, verifies the policy, and approves the deletion.  <- docs/global-commons/ethics.md
[UC] (0.72) INT-09647: **Purpose:** Define the cooldown period after a violation before the same action can be attempted again.  <- docs/global-commons/ethics.md

[AD] (0.78) INT-09649: **When it's assigned:** Request is missing required information (e.g., rationale when `requires_rationale: true`)  <- docs/global-commons/governance.md
[CON] (0.78) INT-09650: **Why:** These are **critical scenarios** where automatic retries are inappropriate. A human must explicitly approve or deny.  <- docs/global-commons/governance.md
[GP] (0.82) INT-09651: **Principle:** Trust requires visibility. If governance rules are secret, users cannot verify they work.  <- docs/global-commons/governance.md
